title: Psychotria edanoii
taxonomy: Psychotria edanoii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is native to Madagascar and is part of the Psychotria genus, which comprises over 1,800 species of plants. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The plants within this family are found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide and exhibit a wide range of growth habits and characteristics. Psychotria edanoii's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Rubiaceae family.

intro:
Unfortunately, there is no information on the plant Psychotria edanoii. However, I can provide you with information on Psychotria viridis, a plant that is closely related and shares similar characteristics.

Psychotria viridis is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as a vine of the forest or chacruna. This plant has been traditionally used in shamanic rituals and medicine in the Amazon rainforest, where it is believed to offer various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved cognitive function, and relief from anxiety and depression. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria viridis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Psychotria viridis is often made into a tea or used in combination with other plants to create preparations such as ayahuasca, a traditional Amazonian brew used for spiritual and medicinal purposes.
